objective: Audit the portfolio target read-only and produce an evidence-backed project profile and module inventory as structured JSON facts; write nothing to product code or memory.

projectContext.query: project profile stack modules architecture entrypoints commands app components lib data

acceptanceCriteria: Profile facts (language, runtime/engines, framework+version, package manager, key npm scripts, high-level architecture) are each accompanied by concrete evidence: a file path with line or a package.json field.

A module inventory covers every real top-level source area (app router incl. api, components/site, components/avatar, lib, data, public assets) with responsibility, entrypoints, relevant commands, dependencies/external services, security boundaries, key files, related tests (or 'none'), and a status of verified|hypothesis.
The realtime-session API route and any .env.local usage are described as security/data boundaries WITHOUT reproducing any secret value or env content.
Every fact carries evidence; anything not verifiable from source, package.json, or the company-map index is omitted or marked hypothesis.
No product file and no memory file is modified by this stage (read-only audit).
constraints: Read-only: do not use Edit/Write on any product file or any .company/memory file. Return facts only inside your AGENT_REPORT JSON.
Read only the files you need; start from projectContext.relevantFiles and expand only from concrete import/usage evidence (Constitution rule 11).
Use scripts/company-map query|symbol|affected portfolio for index-backed evidence; do NOT load .company/index/nodes.jsonl or edges.jsonl wholesale.
Never copy secret values or machine-absolute paths into your facts. Refer to files by repo-relative path.
deliverables: A profileFacts array (each: fact, value, evidence) inside decisionsProposed/summary or an explicit facts block.
A moduleInventory array (each following the modules.md template fields) with evidence and status.
An explicit note on security boundaries (realtime-session route, env usage).
